The platform strives to be user-friendly and aims to provide a hassle-free experience for its users. With Smartcare Diagnostics, individuals can easily navigate through different blood test options, compare prices, and make informed decisions based on their budget and requirements.

Name: ImmunoGI
Description:
Cell-mediated food sensitivity is a form of allergic reaction to different food proteins. It differs in immune mechanism and symptomology from the classical and most commonly known antibody-mediated (IgE) food allergy. A food-LTT can diagnose whether your immune cells (lymphocytes) are sensitized to specific proteins present in common foods we consume. 

Food-LTT is a blood test that measures your immune cell responses to different food proteins. It can help identify which specific foods in your diet may be causing excessive intestinal immune reactivity and untoward digestive symptoms. Determining which specific foods induce high cell-mediated reactivity can help determine what diet is better suited for your specific condition.

Our Vision

What makes True Code unique, exclusive partners for the following leading edge tests.

TruCheck Early Cancer Screening Blood Test

The TruCheck Early Cancer Screening blood test is a thorough check for the existence of cancer in your body, identifying which type of cancer(s) you have, and from which organ they have originated.

The TruCheck Intelli Early Multi-Cancer Screening blood test checks your blood sample for Circulating Tumour Cells (CTCs), and clusters of the cells. New in the UK, this test is revolutionising the ability to detect cancer at its earliest stages, and we are proud to be one of the first providers in the country to offer it to the British public. A non-invasive procedure, this test requires a simple blood test appointment to draw a small sample from a vein in your arm. This sample is then analysed at a leading UK laboratory to detect the existence of any CTCs and therefore to determine whether you have any cancers currently in your body.

Name: Fiji Institute of Medical Laboratory Sciences
Description:
In March 1983 few medical laboratory expertise and consultant pathologists spearheaded by Mr. Satish Sudhakar met at the Pathology Department, Colonial War Memorial Hospital and formed an organization called the “Fiji Medical Laboratory Technologists Association” (FMLTA).

This was the birth of our first ever professional association. The first ever seminar was also held this year at the CWMH Antenatal clinic room.In 2011 the proposed name was put forward to change the naming right of the association from FMLTA to Fiji Institute of Medical Laboratory Science (FIMLS) and was finally endorsed by the members in 2012. An interim committee was formed that will take FIMLS through this transition period. 

These heroes displayed uncommon courage and foresight as they pursued not only career and welfare issues, but also to improve the standards, status, education and training of members of the profession. 

FIMLS is a non profitable organisation that is funded entirely by membership dues and revenues from goods and services. Our members practice in hospital laboratories, private medical laboratories, public health laboratories, research and educational institutions. 

With the inclusion of the phlebotomist, and the passing of the Allied Heath Practitioners (AHP) Bill, FIMLS has since then been growing from strength to strength.

Many advanced countries routinely test for Group B Strep, but the UK does not. A Group B Strep trial is underway in the UK and the results will be used to inform the future of pregnancy care across the United Kingdom but until testing is offered universally the risks of this damaging infection remain and it is the task of private providers such as Strepelle to supply the highest quality testing and support services to pregnant mothers.

Name: Brainstorm Diagnostics
Description:
Concussion (known also as mild traumatic brain injury- mTBI) is the most common diagnosis of brain injury in the United States (over 3 million annually) as well as globally. Public awareness is most significant in sports related concussion. The diagnosis of a concussion is determined by clinical criteria, rather than a specific identified pathology. This lack of precision is reflected in the outcome after concussion, where 80% of individuals have a rapid recovery, in less than a week, but 20% have a delayed recovery that can last months, despite both groups having an identical clinical presentation.  Imaging, either CT or MRI, has not provided a distinguishing signature to differentiate the groups but blood biomarkers that detect damage in the connections between brain cells, the axons, is very promising. In particular, a molecule known as spectrin N terminal fragment (SNTF),  has shown to be highly predictive in separating the two populations when tested in clinical trials of athletes or concussed persons in ER settings within 72 hours of injury. 

 Brainstorm was founded in 2023 to commercialize the use of SNTF for detecting individuals who have had axonal injury after concussion and are much more likely to have  delayed recovery. The technology is patent protected  with an exclusive license to Brainstorm from the University of Pennsylvania.  The founders are two Penn faculty, Dr. Douglas Smith, a neuroscientist and director of the Center for Brain Injury and Repair and Dr. Sean Grady, former chair of the Department of Neurosurgery, and Dr. Matt Tormenti, CEO of Brainstorm and a neurosurgeon in private practice. We are part of the Penn UPstart program that helps faculty commercialize their research.

Unlike big, factory laboratories, the Harmony team knows every clinician we work with personally, and is able to craft each restoration to dentist specifications for easy placement.

We focus on three key points to aid us in ensuring every case and every clinician receives the best care possible.

1. Relationships
The Harmony Dental Creations team will go the extra mile for our clients. Due to our smaller team, your cases will be handled by the same technicians every time. You are never a number, which means you can be confident that you and your patient will be taken care of when you partner with Harmony. We value your time and are ready to work with you to ensure every case ends with superior results.

2. Transparency
Every restoration you receive from our team is proudly crafted in America. Transparency is vital to a long-lasting and beneficial practice-lab partnership. Many laboratories nowadays are outsourcing to overseas shops, which are not as strictly regulated as American labs. We never outsource overseas and hold the fabrication of our restorations and prosthetics to extremely high standards.

3. Ownership
Our laboratory’s golden rule is to treat every case as though the final restoration is going into our own mouths. This ownership of the restoration means that we take as much time and care with every case as needed to ensure exceptional results. This high level of commitment means you and your patient are getting the very best restoration for every case.
